title: marsilea minuta var. incurva
intro: Marsilea minuta var. incurva, also known as the small waterclover or common waterclover, is a species of aquatic plant in the family Marsileaceae, native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. This plant is used in traditional medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, which contribute to its potential in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Marsilea minuta var. incurva include its ability to aid in the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential in reducing inflammation and improving wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
